subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Kepler's Laws of Planetary Motion
description: A quick, practical guide to Kepler's Laws of Planetary Motion.

hook.text: Johannes Kepler discovered that planets orbit in ellipses, not circles. It took him years of calculation to prove it.

buildup.text: Using Tycho's data, Kepler tried circular orbits first. They didn't fit Mars by 8 arc-minutes—and he refused to ignore it.

discovery.text: His three laws describe how planets move: elliptical orbits, equal areas in equal times, and a period-distance relationship.

twist.text: Kepler was motivated by mysticism—he believed God designed the cosmos with geometric harmony. His math outlasted his theology.

climax.text: Newton later showed Kepler's laws follow directly from gravity. Kepler described the motion; Newton explained why.

punchline.text: Eight arc-minutes of error. Kepler refused to ignore it and found the truth.

quiz.question: What did Kepler discover about planetary orbits?
options: They are ellipses, not perfect circles
They are perfect circles centered on the Sun
They follow random paths influenced by other planets
